summary: electron-orbit: typosquat with postinstall env staging and environment-keyed covert beacon over hand-rolled TLS
details: The package name impersonates the well-known 'electron' brand. install.js, registered as a postinstall hook in package.json, constructs the identifiers 'process' and 'env' via an XOR/charcode routine (__s(key, data)) and writes JSON.stringify(globalThis.process.env) into a marker file under os.tmpdir() — staging the installer's full environment (CI tokens, cloud credentials, npm tokens, etc.) to disk while hiding the env access from static review. dist/index.js runs setupAutoRenderOnImport() at module load; this code path resolves a hardcoded remote host only when sha256(process.env.BuildType) matches a hardcoded digest (0ceaa396…), an environment-keyed trigger that activates only on a specific target. The outbound connection is carried by a from-scratch TLS 1.3 client implemented in src/aetherls.ts (ClientHello, P-256 ECDH, AES-128-GCM, custom HKDF) over a raw node:net socket on port 443, bypassing node:https, proxy interception, and EDR/TLS-inspecting network instrumentation. The publicly returned icon data is a static SVG header independent of the network call, confirming the request is a covert side-channel rather than an icon fetch. The same XOR cipher hides 'process', 'env', 'node:net', 'connect', 'GET ', and the destination URL across install.js and src/aetherls.ts. Combination of brand impersonation, postinstall environment staging with obfuscated env access, environment-keyed activation, hand-rolled TLS transport, and import-time outbound I/O is a credential-exfiltration / covert-C2 supply-chain attack against installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
